column1: # Kanripo gaiji ladder — lane 3 of 4: the SUBSTITUTE normalization map (P38-1).
# Rung 3 of the four-rung display ladder documented in kanripo.tsv: consulted
# for a `&KR\d+;` ref ONLY when the FAITHFUL (rung 1) and IDS (rung 2) lanes do
# not resolve it. A hit renders a lossy standard-char normalization — a real but
# NON-faithful glyph (a scholar's read-through), strictly better than the ⬚ box.
# NEVER merged into kanripo.tsv: faithful and substitute are separate lanes so
# the renderer can label the resolution's fidelity. The lanes are DISJOINT by
# ref-id (a ref with a faithful glyph is dropped here; tested).
# SUBSTITUTE POLICY (charlist column 4, "normalized version"): the ref must NOT
# be in the faithful lane, and column 4 must, after stripping whitespace, be
# EXACTLY ONE real assigned codepoint (no Private-Use). Dropped: `?`-uncertain,
# mandoku `[...]` compositions, frequency-list cells (`屎:10,尸@矢:8` — comma),
# the U+3000 ideographic-space "blank" substitute, empties. 45 of these refs
# carry a Private-Use "faithful" cell purged from rung 1 but a clean column-4
# substitute, and are rescued into coverage here.
# census: 562, 2026-07-21, substitute refs — 45.25% of all 1,751,360 gaiji
# The P37-3 header's ~52% estimate counted all 707 column-4-only refs incl. the
# 190 messy cells (uncertain / compositions / frequency-lists) not shippable as
# a single glyph; the verified single-glyph figure is 45.25%.
